title: AFI Provider Strategy Binding v1 (FACTORY-CONTRACT — provider-to-strategy routing contract)
description: The canonical provider-to-strategy binding (afi.provider-strategy-binding.v1): which STRATEGIES an authenticated signal provider may route into, and which one is the default when the inbound signal does not select one. A binding names the provider, its ingress class (providerType), the authentication CLASS that authenticates it (authenticatedBy — never secret material), the whitelist of canonical strategy triples it may invoke (allowedStrategies), an optional defaultStrategy (which MUST be a member of allowedStrategies), and an active/inactive status.
$comment: BOUNDARY (FACTORY-CONTRACT): schema/contract only — NO route implementation, NO tenant management, NO secret storage. authenticatedBy names the mechanism class (route-secret | gateway-tenant | integration-key); the secret/tenant/key VALUES live in operator configuration, never here. The defaultStrategy-membership rule is cross-field over array membership (not JSON-Schema-draft-07-enforceable) and is a governed contract constraint enforced by tooling/tests.
x-afiDoctrineRefs: afi-governance/decisions/factory-configurable-pipelines-v1 — authorizes the canonical V1 contract family
afi-governance/decisions/object-identity-v0.1.md (OBJ-GOV) D-OBJ-3 — allowedStrategies entries are canonical strategy identity triples

reports/afi-configurable-dag-live-runtime-reconciliation.md §1/§6.2 — the resolution seam: today every provider statically resolves to one strategy; this binding makes that routing declarative and auditable

x-afiConstraints.defaultMembership: CROSS-FIELD (not JSON-Schema-draft-07-enforceable): defaultStrategy, when present, MUST be deep-equal to one of the allowedStrategies entries. Enforced by tooling/tests; a binding violating it fails closed.
x-afiConstraints.noSecretMaterial: authenticatedBy names the authentication CLASS only. Secret values, tenant records, and integration keys are operator configuration and MUST NOT appear in this artifact (additionalProperties:false leaves them nowhere to live).
x-afiConstraints.strategyResolution: REGISTRY-TOOLING: every allowedStrategies triple SHOULD resolve to an ACTIVE afi.analyst-strategy-registration.v1 entry whose providerBindingPolicy admits this binding (mode 'any-authenticated', or 'explicit' listing this bindingId). Routing through a non-resolving triple fails closed at composition time.
x-afiConstraints.strategyIdMajorAgreement: Each triple's strategyId embedded major MUST equal its strategyVersion major (D-OBJ-3; tooling-enforced).

schema.description: Schema-id version of the provider strategy binding.

bindingId.description: Stable identifier of this binding (referenced by registration providerBindingPolicy.allowedBindings).

providerId.description: Stable identifier of the signal provider (same id space as USS provenance.providerId).

providerType.description: Ingress class: 'webhook' (e.g. TradingView alert webhook), 'cpj' (Chat Parse JSON ingestion), 'gateway' (afi-gateway tenant ingress).

authenticatedBy.description: The authentication CLASS that authenticates this provider at ingress. Names the mechanism only — never carries the secret value (x-afiConstraints.noSecretMaterial).

allowedStrategies.description: Whitelist of canonical strategy identity triples this provider may route into.

defaultStrategy.description: OPTIONAL default triple used when the inbound signal does not select a strategy. MUST be a member of allowedStrategies (x-afiConstraints.defaultMembership).

status.description: Binding status. Only 'active' bindings route; 'inactive' retires without deletion.
